authoritative_sources.title: SSA POMS DI 23000 Disability and SSI Process Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines SSA disability evidence structure, assessment flow, and required documentation posture for disability-related intake and triage.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Medicaid Eligibility
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal workflow guide for Medicaid intake and verification sequencing that directly drives eligibility readiness expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicaid and CHIP Enrollment and Redetermination
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: States the practical intake and verification workflow, renewal, and ongoing evidence maintenance expectations used for intake hold and escalation logic.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS HCBS Waiver Reference (1915(c))
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides the federal baseline for community-based service access pathways and waiver-like eligibility prerequisites used in elder-care and disability delivery systems.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule: Minimum Necessary
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines information-use and sharing constraints that govern intake intake, consent checks, and role-based access.

workflow_stages.name: intake packet normalization
workflow_stages.description: Ingest and normalize referral or support requests, actor role, and tenant context into a structured intake shape for eligibility-lane routing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: prerequisite and evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Validate identity, residency, age/disability context, consent, and evidence completeness before downstream execution can begin.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: authorization and access checks
workflow_stages.description: Confirm reservations, prior authorization, and access control prerequisites and produce escalation when any precondition is missing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: privacy and routing decision
workflow_stages.description: Apply consent and record-sharing controls before routing or producing any handoff output.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: escalation and conflict handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate conflicting guidance, jurisdiction gaps, or uncertain evidence rather than resolving beyond lane scope.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Intake request summary and referral payloads

allowed: Validate prerequisite and intake completeness before downstream execution
Route wrong-lane or low-confidence requests to appropriate specialist lanes
Escalate source conflicts and policy ambiguity with explicit assumptions
escalate: Missing tenant-specific jurisdiction or rule set
Contradictory public guidance versus tenant policy
Consent or privacy ambiguity for records sharing
Clinical or legal requests outside intake scope
refuse: Request to approve or deny formal benefit entitlement
Request to provide legal advice on appeals
Request to provide safety-critical service authorization

domain_failure_modes: Case enters execution with missing identity, residency, or authorization context.
State policy differences materially change outcome and are treated as missing context.
Conflicting disability or access guidance is ignored and replaced by generic assumptions.
Third-party requests bypass consent and minimum-necessary controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State- or program-specific age and disability eligibility variants
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could materially alter which evidence and cutoff logic apply.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Route for tenant/state policy retrieval and block final conclusions until confirmed.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific consent role maps and authority conventions
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May change whether data release and routing are allowed.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use orchestrator-provided policy set and keep unknowns escalated.

notes: The lane is materially constrained to intake and access validation and does not claim downstream service entitlement authority.
Conflicting policy and evidence conditions are represented as escalation artifacts instead of resolved at intake.
No blocking ambiguity is accepted for validated execution because unresolved points generate explicit escalation outputs.
